channel.title: Posts on mlesniak.com
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on mlesniak.com

item.title: Go Guitar // Visualize Sound
item.description: After understanding how to record sound in Go I wanted to visualize the recorded data in realtime instead of having solely a static image. The graphics library SDL is available on all major platforms, well documented, and in particular has (at least) one go binding with good examples. The full code for this example can be found here. In this article I’ll focus on setting up and using SDL in Go and describe a pitfall I ran into.

item.title: Go Guitar // Record Sound
item.description: As part of my ongoing project to write a Go program to recognize different guitar chords and get a better understanding of sound processing, a first milestone has been reached: recording a raw sound sample in Go. In this article I’ll walk through the source code to record sounds using a wrapper for PortAudio and visualize the recorded sample in gnuplot. The whole source code for this blog post can be found in the corresponding GitHub repository.

item.title: Shortcut Links in zsh
item.description: ZSH is my favourite shell, and while it comes with a lot of features out of the box and is even more powerful with oh-my-zsh that does not mean that my daily workflow can not be improved even more. A situation I’m facing often is to switch between multiple directories. While the autojump plugin exists I have to admit that I dislike to type the necessary prefix. Hence, the shell function link was created:

item.title: A curated list of some GitHub projects
item.description: One of my philosophies is release early, make a fool of yourself quickly, improve and iterate. Hence I put many of my smaller and larger projects on GitHub. To be honest, most of them are left in an unfinished state: after I’ve solved the challenging problem, the project is mostly left for future reference. Some projects which are particularly interesting are listed below: port-scanner is a port-scanner written in Go and one of the first non-toy Go projects I’ve written.

item.title: Contact
item.description: Interested in discussions? Curious about my other projects? Simply use one of the following channels to get in touch with me… GitHub Email Xing LinkedIn Twitter
